general_notes: All compounds are cuprate high-temperature superconductors. Tc is the bulk superconducting transition temperature in Kelvin measured at ambient pressure on the optimal-doping stoichiometry. Year of first synthesis refers to the first peer-reviewed report of superconductivity in that compound class. Sources: original discovery papers, Reviews of Modern Physics, and standard solid-state physics references.

search_groups.description: Cuprate compound formula with common abbreviation.
Critical temperature and year of first synthesis from condensed matter physics literature.

validation_targets.description: Cuprate compound formula with common abbreviation in parentheses.
validation_targets.notes: Use the full formula for identification. Abbreviation in parentheses (e.g. YBCO, Bi-2223).

validation_targets.description: Bulk onset superconducting transition temperature in Kelvin at ambient pressure, optimal doping.
validation_targets.notes: Integer or one decimal place. For YBCO: 93 K. For LSCO: 38 K. Report the widely cited consensus value.

validation_targets.description: Year the first peer-reviewed discovery paper was published reporting superconductivity in this compound class.
validation_targets.notes: 4-digit year. Sources: original discovery papers and Reviews of Modern Physics.
